codeReview.summary: Clean, well-scoped fix. Added CpuBackend::update_bounds_for_skip() (28 lines) that zeroes stale prev_bounds entries when node count decreases during skipped frames and updates prev_node_count. Compositor's Skip path now calls this before releasing the scene buffer. 5 new TDD tests (373 lines) thoroughly cover single-skip, multi-skip, node-count-decrease, stale-entry-zeroing, and pixel-correctness scenarios. All verification steps passed (1,791 tests, clean build, TRIPLE_SCENE_SIZE confirmed in init). The implementation is correct and the API design is sound — centralizing Skip-side bookkeeping in a single method is good for maintainability. One non-blocking observation: the zeroing branch inside update_bounds_for_skip (n < old_n) is currently unreachable in production because prepare_frame() returns Full (not Skip) whenever node_count != prev_node_count. The prev_node_count update is also redundant since prepare_frame's Skip paths already set it. This makes update_bounds_for_skip purely defensive/future-proofing today, which is acceptable — it documents the invariant and provides a single place to extend Skip-side bookkeeping if prepare_frame's logic evolves.

issues.description: The zeroing branch (n < old_n) in update_bounds_for_skip is currently unreachable: prepare_frame() returns FrameAction::Full whenever node_count != self.prev_node_count (line 218), so Skip is only returned when counts match, making n == old_n always true. Similarly, the prev_node_count assignment (line 356) is redundant since prepare_frame's Skip paths already set it (lines 226, 263). This is defensive code, not a bug — it's correct if prepare_frame's guard ever changes. Consider adding a brief comment noting the defensive nature, e.g. '// Defensive: currently unreachable since prepare_frame guards on count change, but protects against future changes.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The new update_bounds_for_skip() method is not mentioned in .factory/library/architecture.md's 'Damage Tracking — PREV_BOUNDS' section, which describes the prev_bounds lifecycle (on render, on partial update). The section should be updated to include the Skip-frame bookkeeping path to keep the architecture doc complete.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md documents prev_bounds lifecycle for render and partial-update paths but has no mention of the Skip path or update_bounds_for_skip(). New method at system/libraries/render/lib.rs:331-357.

summary: PASS. The fix correctly adds a dedicated update_bounds_for_skip() method to CpuBackend and integrates it into the compositor's Skip path. The core logic (zeroing stale entries, updating prev_node_count) is defensive — currently unreachable due to prepare_frame's existing guard — but provides a clean API boundary for Skip-side bookkeeping. Tests are thorough and well-structured, covering all 5 expected behaviors (VAL-DMG-001 through VAL-DMG-004, VAL-INIT-001). No blocking issues. One non-blocking observation about defensive/unreachable code, and one shared-state observation about updating architecture docs.
